🟣 Terraform ≥ 1.5.0 🟠 AWS us-east-1 🔵 HCL 100% ✅ Local + Remote Backend

AWS Terraform Cloud
Infrastructure Project

A complete AWS learning project that provisions a production-grade VPC network, EC2 instances, Auto Scaling Groups, and S3 storage — with full Terraform Cloud remote state management and a local backend fallback.

8HCL Files
2Local Modules
6Subnets
4EC2 Instances
2ASGs
4Security Groups
30Screenshots
🏗️ Architecture ⚙️ Prerequisites 📁 Structure 📋 Resources 🔄 Workflow 🖥️ Terraform Steps ☁️ AWS Console 🗑️ Destroy 🗄️ Backends 🔒 Security
1
Infrastructure Architecture
— Complete AWS topology provisioned by Terraform
📐 AWS Architecture Diagram — us-east-1
ℹ️
Region: us-east-1 · VPC CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 · Public Subnets: 10.0.101–103.0/24 · Private Subnets: 10.0.1–3.0/24 · Variables Subnet: 10.0.250.0/24 · All instances are Ubuntu 20.04 (Canonical AMI, owner: 099720109477)
2
Prerequisites
— What you need before running this project
🔧
Terraform CLI ≥ 1.5.0
Install from developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/downloads. Verify with terraform version.
🔐
AWS Credentials
Configure via aws configure or set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY as env vars.
🛡️
IAM Permissions
VPC, EC2, EIP/NAT, S3, IAM, AutoScaling, KMS (if encrypting state). Least privilege recommended.
☁️
Terraform Cloud (optional)
Create org + CLI-driven workspace at app.terraform.io. Run terraform login to authenticate.
💻
PowerShell (Windows only)
Used by the local-exec provisioner to set PEM file permissions via icacls. Comment out on Linux/macOS.
📦
Git
The remote-exec provisioner clones hashicorp/demo-terraform-101 onto the EC2 instance at boot.
3
Project File Structure
— Refactored from a 460-line monolith into focused files
terraform-refactored/ ├── terraform.tf # Provider requirements · required_version · backend config ├── variables.tf # All 12 input variables with types, defaults, validation ├── locals.tf # Data sources (AZs, AMI, region) + shared local values ← NEW ├── networking.tf # VPC · 7 subnets · IGW · EIP · NAT GW · route tables ← NEW ├── security.tf # 4 security groups — isolated from compute ← NEW ├── keypair.tf # TLS RSA key · local_file PEM · aws_key_pair · random_id ← NEW ├── compute.tf # 2 EC2 resources + 4 modules (server, web, ASG×2) ← NEW ├── storage.tf # S3 bucket + ownership controls ← NEW ├── outputs.tf # 10 root outputs centralised (split from main.tf) ├── terraform.tfvars.example # Safe template — copy to terraform.tfvars (gitignored) ├── .gitignore # *.pem · *.tfvars · *.tfstate · crash.log · keys ├── .terraform.lock.hcl # Provider lock file — commit this ├── README.md │ ├── modules/ │ ├── server/ │ │ └── main.tf # Simple EC2 (no SSH provisioner) · out: ip, dns, id │ └── web_server/ │ └── main.tf # EC2 + remote-exec bootstrap · out: ip, dns, id │ └── screenshots/ # 30 proof-of-execution screenshots
4

aws_security_groupingress_sshTCP 22 in · all egresssecurity.tf
aws_security_groupvpc_webTCP 80+443 in · all egress · workspace-namedsecurity.tf
aws_security_groupvpc_pingICMP in · all egresssecurity.tf
tls_private_keygeneratedRSA 4096-bitkeypair.tf
local_fileprivate_key_pemMyTerraformInstanceKey.pem · mode 0600keypair.tf
aws_key_pairgeneratedMyTerraformInstanceKey · public key uploadkeypair.tf
random_idrandomness16-byte hex suffix for S3keypair.tf
aws_instanceterraform_instancet2.micro · public_subnet_1 · remote-exec+local-execcompute.tf
aws_instanceweb_servert2.micro · public_subnet_1 · local var tags democompute.tf
moduleserver./modules/server · public_subnet_3 · no SSHcompute.tf
moduleserver_subnet_1./modules/web_server · public_subnet_1 · SSH+bootstrapcompute.tf
module (registry)autoscalingmyasg_local · private subnets · min:0 max:1compute.tf

aws_s3_bucketlearning_bucketterraform-handson-lab-{hex}storage.tf
aws_s3_bucket_ownership_controlslearning_bucketBucketOwnerEnforcedstorage.tf
5
Deployment Workflow
— Step-by-step Terraform CLI commands
1
terraform init
Downloads providers (aws, tls, local, random, http) and registry modules. Sets up backend.
2
terraform plan
Previews all resources to create. Use -out tfplan to save the plan for deterministic apply.
3
terraform apply
Provisions all AWS infrastructure. EC2 instances run remote-exec bootstrap provisioners.
4
terraform output
Displays public IPs, DNS names, VPC ID, S3 bucket name, subnet IDs, and NAT GW IP.
5
terraform destroy
Tears down all resources in reverse dependency order. ⚠️ Always run when done — billable resources!
bash — Quick Start (Local Backend) 
# 1. Clone and enter the project
git clone https://github.com/DebopriyoRoy/AWS-TERRAFORM-CLOUD
cd AWS-TERRAFORM-CLOUD

# 2. Copy and customise variables
cp terraform.tfvars.example terraform.tfvars

# 3. Initialise providers and modules
terraform init

# 4. Preview the execution plan
terraform plan -out tfplan

# 5. Apply and provision AWS infrastructure
terraform apply tfplan

# 6. Check outputs (IPs, DNS, bucket name)
terraform output

# 7. When finished — destroy all resources!
terraform destroy
6
Terraform Execution — Screenshots
— CLI and Terraform Cloud UI proof-of-execution
01
Remote Backend Init
🔌 terraform init connecting to Terraform Cloud remote backend at app.terraform.io · org: Personal-Terraform-cloud · workspace: my-aws-app · Downloads all 5 providers and the autoscaling registry module.
02
Terraform Plan — CLI Output
📋 terraform plan running from CLI — shows resources to be created, modified, or destroyed. Plan is streamed to Terraform Cloud for policy checks before apply is allowed.
03
Terraform Cloud — Plan Finished
✅ Plan finished on Terraform Cloud UI — all resources validated, cost estimation displayed, Sentinel policy checks pending.
04
Terraform Cloud — Policy Checks
🛡️ Policy checks (Sentinel) running on Terraform Cloud — validates the plan against organisational compliance rules before apply is permitted.
05
Terraform Cloud — Apply Running
🚀 terraform apply in progress on Terraform Cloud — resources being created in dependency order: VPC → Subnets → IGW → NAT → EC2 instances.
06
Apply Complete — CLI Output
✅ Apply complete! All resources created successfully. CLI displays final outputs including public IP, public URL, VPC information, and module outputs.
07
Terraform Cloud — Apply Finished
🎉 Apply finished on Terraform Cloud — run completed successfully, state file updated remotely, all resources tracked.
08
Terraform Cloud — Outputs
📤 Outputs tab in Terraform Cloud — displays all root module outputs: terraform_instance_public_ip, public_url, vpc_id, module IPs/DNS, S3 bucket name.
09
Terraform Cloud — Resources
📋 Resources tab in Terraform Cloud — full inventory of all provisioned resources tracked in remote state with their current status.
10
Terraform Cloud — Runs List
📜 Runs history on Terraform Cloud — shows all plan/apply/destroy runs with status, trigger source, and timestamps for full audit trail.
7
AWS Console — Provisioned Resources
— Live proof of all infrastructure created in us-east-1
11
EC2 Instances
🖥️ EC2 Instances — terraform_instance, web_server (direct resources) + Server from Module, Web Server from Module (from local modules). All t2.micro · Ubuntu 20.04 in public subnets.
12
VPC List
🌐 VPC — demo_vpc created with CIDR 10.0.0.0/16 in us-east-1. Tagged with Environment=demo_environment, Terraform=true, ManagedBy=Terraform.
13
Security Groups
🔒 Security Groups — all 4 created: web_server_inbound (TCP 443), allow-all-ssh (TCP 22), vpc-web-default (TCP 80+443), vpc-ping (ICMP). All attached to demo_vpc.
14
EBS Volumes
💾 EBS Volumes — root volumes automatically attached to each EC2 instance. Default 8GiB gp2 volumes per instance, one per EC2 instance in the public subnets.
15
Internet Gateway
🚪 Internet Gateway — demo_igw attached to demo_vpc. Enables internet access for all public subnets. Required for the public route table's default 0.0.0.0/0 route.
16
Subnets
🗺️ Subnets — 7 subnets created: 3 public (10.0.101–103.0/24), 3 private (10.0.1–3.0/24), 1 variables-subnet (10.0.250.0/24). Spread across us-east-1a/b/c.
17
NAT Gateway
🔄 NAT Gateway — demo_nat_gateway deployed in public_subnet_1 with Elastic IP. Enables private subnet instances to reach the internet for updates without being publicly reachable.
18
Route Tables
🗂️ Route Tables — demo_public_rtb (0.0.0.0/0 → IGW) associated with 3 public subnets; demo_private_rtb (0.0.0.0/0 → NAT GW) associated with 3 private subnets.
19
Elastic IPs
🏷️ Elastic IP — demo_igw_eip allocated and associated with the NAT Gateway. Provides a static public IP for outbound traffic from private subnet instances.
20
Network ACLs
🛡️ Network ACLs — default NACLs in place (allow all inbound/outbound). These are stateless and operate at the subnet level, complementing stateful Security Groups.
21
Auto Scaling Groups
📈 Auto Scaling Groups — myasg_local and myasg_github deployed in private subnets via the terraform-aws-modules/autoscaling registry module (v8.0.1). min:0, max:1, desired:1.
22
EC2 Dashboard
📊 EC2 Dashboard — summary view showing all running instances, key pairs, security groups, and volumes across the region. Confirms full provisioning.
23
S3 Bucket
🪣 S3 Bucket — terraform-handson-lab-{random_hex} created with BucketOwnerEnforced ownership controls. Tagged Name=Terraform-learning-bucket · Purpose=Intro to Resource Blocks Lab.
8
Backend Migration & Destroy
— Migrating to local backend, then tearing down all resources
24
Migrate to Local Backend — Init
🔄 terraform init -reconfigure — migrating state from Terraform Cloud back to local backend. The backend block in terraform.tf is commented out and terraform re-inits with local state.
25
Local Backend — State List
📋 terraform state list — confirms all resources are tracked in local terraform.tfstate after migration from remote backend. All resources visible and intact.
26
Terraform Destroy — In Progress
🗑️ terraform destroy running — resources being destroyed in reverse dependency order. EC2 instances first, then NAT GW, then EIP, then subnets, then VPC.
27
Terraform Destroy — Complete
✅ Destroy complete! All resources destroyed and deregistered. State file updated to empty. Zero AWS resources remain — no further billing.
28
EC2 Dashboard After Destroy
🧹 EC2 Dashboard after destroy — all instances terminated, key pairs removed, security groups deleted. Dashboard confirms clean state with no running resources.
29
VPC Dashboard After Destroy
🧹 VPC Dashboard after destroy — demo_vpc deleted, all subnets removed, IGW detached and deleted, NAT GW and EIP released. Only the default VPC remains.
30
S3 Buckets Empty After Destroy
🧹 S3 after destroy — terraform-handson-lab-{hex} bucket deleted. S3 console confirms no project buckets remain. All storage resources cleaned up.
9
Backend Configuration
— Local backend vs Terraform Cloud remote backend
🏠 Local Backend (Default)
terraform.tf
# Default — no backend block needed
# State stored in ./terraform.tfstate
terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5.0"
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = ">=5.82.1, <6.0.0"
    }
  }
}
✅
Supports local-exec (PowerShell), local_file, and remote-exec provisioners. Best for local development.
☁️ Terraform Cloud (Remote)
terraform.tf — uncomment to enable
terraform {
  backend "remote" {
    hostname = "app.terraform.io"
    organization = "Personal-Terraform-cloud"
    workspaces {
      name = "my-aws-app"
    }
  }
}

# Then run:
# terraform login
# terraform init -reconfigure
⚠️
Use Local execution mode in the workspace — local-exec and local_file require local file system access. Set AWS credentials as workspace env vars.
10
Security Notes
— What to harden before production use
⚠️
All Security Groups use 0.0.0.0/0 — restrict ingress CIDR blocks to known IP ranges for production workloads. Especially TCP 22 (SSH).
⚠️
Private key in Terraform state — tls_private_key stores the RSA key in state. Use AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault for production key management.
💡
Enable S3 public access block — uncomment aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block in storage.tf to fully privatise the bucket for non-public workloads.
✅
myasg_github already uses SSM — the github ASG has AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore attached, enabling session-based access without opening port 22.
💰
Billable AWS resources — NAT Gateways, EC2 instances, Elastic IPs, and S3 all incur charges while running. Always run terraform destroy when done with the lab.
AWS-TERRAFORM-CLOUD · DebopriyoRoy · github.com/DebopriyoRoy/AWS-TERRAFORM-CLOUD
Terraform ≥ 1.5.0 · AWS us-east-1 · HCL 100% · 30 screenshots · terraform-aws-modules/autoscaling v8.0.1
⚠️ Run terraform destroy when done — billable resources
